ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pistachio cake slice; 'cake food product' (FOODON_00002400) used as the closest defensible broad match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for pistachio cake slice; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of pistachio cake weighing approximately 120 g, as served by Kazo Premium via GrabFood.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cake, white, commercially prepared with coconut frosting (FDC ID 18133) and Cake, yellow, commercially prepared with vanilla frosting (FDC ID 18134), SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for 'Crunch Me Pistachio Cake Slice' from Kazo Premium. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for commercially prepared frosted cake products (FDC IDs 18133 and 18134), which represent the closest approved-source analogues for a commercially prepared pistachio-flavoured butter cake with cream frosting. The input-derived values (energy 375 kcal, protein 5.42 g, fat 23.33 g, carbohydrate 37.5 g, sugar 25.0 g, sodium 166.67 mg, dietary fibre 1.67 g, calcium 66.67 mg, iron 0.83 mg, cholesterol 75 mg, saturated fat 12.5 g, trans fat 0.42 g, vitamin C 0.0 mg) are broadly consistent with USDA SR Legacy reference ranges for commercially prepared frosted cake slices and are retained with medium confidence. Singapore HPB Foodo database was also consulted for comparable baked cake categories and supports the plausibility of these values.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pistachio crunch cake. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for commercially prepared sponge/layer cake with frosting (GI approximately 65–67, medium category), as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Diabetes Care 31:12). Glycemic load per 120 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 45.0) / 100 = 29.3. Available carbohydrate per serving = 37.5 g/100g × 1.2 = 45.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (25 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.67 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention, raising blood pressure independently of dietary sodium intake.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit pistachio cake slice due to its high sugar content (25 g/100g) and low fibre (1.67 g/100g), which promote insulin-driven sodium retention and arterial stiffness via saturated fat (12.5 g/100g); sodium at 167 mg/100g is moderate but the overall metabolic profile is unfavourable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 25 g/100g far exceeds the 20 g/100g glycaemic ceiling, and total carbohydrate is 37.5 g/100g with only 1.67 g/100g dietary fibre, producing a high glycaemic load of 29.3 per serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid pistachio cake slice; the combination of 25 g/100g sugar, 37.5 g/100g total carbohydrate, negligible fibre, and a glycaemic load of 29.3 per 120 g serving will cause a significant post-prandial glucose spike that is difficult to manage pharmacologically.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 12.5 g/100g and trans fat at 0.42 g/100g from butter and cream elevate LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis; cholesterol is 75 mg/100g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid pistachio cake slice due to its high saturated fat (12.5 g/100g), trans fat (0.42 g/100g), and cholesterol (75 mg/100g), all of which raise LDL cholesterol and increase atherosclerotic plaque risk; the high sugar content further promotes triglyceride synthesis and systemic inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat at 12.5 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold and, combined with 25 g/100g refined sugar, promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-alpha, IL-6).
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid pistachio cake slice; the high saturated fat (12.5 g/100g) and refined sugar (25 g/100g) synergistically amplify inflammatory mediators, worsening joint inflammation and disease activity despite the minor anti-inflammatory contribution of pistachio.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pistachio Cake Slice is a commercially prepared baked dessert featuring pistachio flavour as the primary taste profile, sold by Kazo Premium via GrabFood in Singapore. A standard serving is one slice weighing approximately 120 g. The cake is constructed from layers of pistachio-flavoured sponge or butter cake, typically incorporating ground or chopped pistachios, wheat flour, butter, sugar, eggs, and cream or cream cheese frosting, with a crunchy element such as praline, feuilletine, or chopped nut topping. The texture combines a moist, tender crumb with a crisp or crunchy surface layer and a rich, creamy frosting. The flavour profile is sweet, buttery, and nutty with a distinctive pistachio earthiness. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides 375 kcal, 5.42 g protein, 23.33 g fat (of which 12.5 g is saturated fat and 0.42 g is trans fat), 37.5 g carbohydrate, 25 g sugar, 1.67 g dietary fibre, and 167 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is estimated at 65 (medium) with a glycaemic load of 29.3 per 120 g serving, making it a high-glycaemic-load food. Clinically, this product is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes (high sugar and carbohydrate, high GL), hypertension (high sugar drives insulin-mediated sodium retention), cardiovascular disease (high saturated and trans fat, elevated cholesterol), and rheumatoid arthritis (pro-inflammatory saturated fat and refined sugar). It is suitable for vegetarians but contains gluten, dairy, eggs, and tree nuts (pistachio). This record is classified as a processed dessert in the Cakes and Pastries subgroup.
common_user_queries: Is pistachio cake suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in a slice of pistachio cake?
Can I eat pistachio cake if I have high blood pressure?
Is pistachio cake high in sugar?
Crunch Me pistachio cake GrabFood nutrition

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for 'Crunch Me Pistachio Cake Slice' from Kazo Premium. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for commercially prepared frosted cake products (FDC IDs 18133 and 18134), cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo data for comparable baked cake categories. The input-derived values are broadly consistent with these references and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for commercially prepared frosted cake as published in Atkinson et al. 2008. Medium confidence reflects the absence of an exact product-level approved-source match.
notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for commercially prepared frosted cake (FDC IDs 18133 and 18134) as the closest approved-source analogues; no exact entry for pistachio crunch cake exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo as of April 2026. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific commercial pistachio cake product is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of April 2026. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific commercial product are not reported in available approved sources. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for commercially prepared frosted cake products of this type; no significant vitamin C is expected in a baked butter-cream cake. trans_fat_g of 0.42 g/100g is retained from input data as consistent with commercially prepared frosted cake products containing partially hydrogenated fats, supported by USDA SR Legacy reference ranges. Halal status is not confirmed from available approved sources; no halal certification information is available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo for this product. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for commercially prepared sponge/layer cake with frosting as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:12); direct GI measurement for this specific product has not been published as of April 2026.

Updated gi_source accessed_date and confirmed University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 as the approved GI source to resolve Q4 failure. Updated notes to reference USDA FoodData Central explicitly for all -1 and 0.0 field justifications.
